summary: Phase 3.B (live GitHub Pages verification of the Phase 3.A.2 sim implementation + scripted-playthrough diagnostic-page extension) complete. The two external acceptance criteria that were unreachable from the sandbox during Phase 3.A.2 have now both been observed green on the public GitHub Actions run for commit e090509 on master and on the live deployment URL. With Phase 3.0, 3.A.1, 3.A.2, and 3.B all approved, Phase 3 (Entity Model, Turn Loop, Combat) is complete and Phase 4 (Procedural Atlas Generator — Planning Gate) is unblocked. Acceptance criteria explicitly verified: (1) the live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ serves the updated diagnostic page including the new #sim-scripted section, the #scripted-run button, the SIM_DIGEST golden constant 321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e, and the three new window flags (__SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, __SIM_OUTCOME__, __SIM_FLOOR_REACHED__); and (2) the cross-runtime Playwright job ran the two new sim assertions (tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts:91-105 'scripted-playthrough section runs and reports the SIM_DIGEST' and tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts:107-123 'scripted-playthrough button is idempotent — re-clicking produces same hash') across chromium + firefox + webkit and reported success.
suggested_commit_message: phase-3.B: live deploy + cross-runtime Playwright sim scripted-playthrough verified green

verification_sprint.purpose: Re-confirm the Phase 3.A.2 foundation has not drifted before approving the Phase 3 verification gate. Sandbox-side reproducibility check against the deployed bundle.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 563 passed (vitest run --coverage), 35 test files — same totals as documented at HEAD of Phase 3.A.2; no drift.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green; dist/assets/index-Bx1CgJqA.js = 38.91 KB raw / 15.06 KB gzipped. The locally-produced bundle filename index-Bx1CgJqA.js matches byte-for-byte the bundle filename served live at /icefall/assets/index-Bx1CgJqA.js, which is direct cross-machine evidence that the deployed artifact is exactly what `vite build` produces from this commit (Vite hashes the asset name from its content). 80% headroom under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 bundle budget.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ fetched (HTTP 200 via the configured custom-domain redirect to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/). The deployed bundle index-Bx1CgJqA.js contains every Phase 3 runtime marker probed: SIM_DIGEST, sim-scripted (section id), scripted-run (button id), scripted-output (dl id), __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, __SIM_OUTCOME__, __SIM_FLOOR_REACHED__, sim-cross-runtime-digest (self-test id), sim-stream-isolation (self-test id), the SIM_DIGEST hex literal 321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e, FLOOR_PREVIEW (Phase 2.A flag carried forward), RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST (Phase 1 flag carried forward), 'self-test passed' (banner copy), 'Run scripted playthrough' (button label), and the frozen roll-domain anchor 'icefall:roll:v1:'. The single source-level identifier SELF_TEST_LOG is renamed by the minifier; the literal 100-element action sequence it holds is still live in the executable code paths and is what the in-browser sim-cross-runtime-digest self-test consumes to compute the asserted hash. Deep-link form http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/#seed=phase3-witness&floor=3 also returns HTTP 200.

deploy_workflow_runs_green_within_5min.evidence: GitHub Actions run 25299079462 for commit e090509 on master: status=completed, conclusion=success. run_started_at 2026-05-04T03:09:20Z → updated_at 2026-05-04T03:10:54Z = 1m 34s end-to-end, well under the 5-minute budget. Both jobs ('build & test' 03:09:22→03:10:40 = 1m 18s; 'deploy to GitHub Pages' 03:10:43→03:10:53 = 10s) succeeded with every required step green: lint ✓, typecheck ✓, unit tests + coverage gate ✓, build ✓, bundle size budget (≤ 75 KB gzipped, memo decision 13) ✓, install playwright browsers ✓, cross-runtime e2e ✓, Run actions/deploy-pages@v4 ✓.

live_url_serves_updated_diagnostic_with_.evidence: curl -sSL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ → HTTP 200 (resolves through configured custom-domain CNAME to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/). index.html references /icefall/assets/index-Bx1CgJqA.js, which is the byte-identical content-hashed bundle that the local `vite build` reproduces from this commit. The bundle contains every Phase 3.A.2 marker: SIM_DIGEST + literal 321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e, sim-scripted/scripted-run/scripted-output DOM ids, __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__/__SIM_OUTCOME__/__SIM_FLOOR_REACHED__ window flags, sim-cross-runtime-digest + sim-stream-isolation self-test ids, the 'Run scripted playthrough' button label, and the 'icefall:roll:v1:' frozen roll-domain anchor (decision memo addendum item B1). Phase 1 + Phase 2 markers (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST, FLOOR_PREVIEW, 'self-test passed') are also present, confirming no carry-over regression.

phase_3b_playwright_scripted_playthrough.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'install playwright browsers' (chromium firefox webkit, --with-deps): success. Step 'cross-runtime e2e' (npm run test:e2e): success. playwright.config.ts:6-8 sets forbidOnly:true and retries:0, and projects:25-37 defines all three engines (chromium/firefox/webkit), so any single-engine failure on any single test would have failed the step. tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts now contains 8 tests (6 carried forward from Phase 1.B + 2.A — self-test green; cross-runtime random-walk digest; build info DEV- fingerprint; Node-side digest match; floor-preview ASCII grid ready; floor-preview deterministic ASCII — plus 2 added by Phase 3.A.2): 'scripted-playthrough section runs and reports the SIM_DIGEST' (asserts window.__SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ === SIM_DIGEST) and 'scripted-playthrough button is idempotent — re-clicking produces same hash' (asserts re-click yields same hash and asserts window.__SIM_OUTCOME__ === 'running'). 8 tests × 3 engines = 24 cross-runtime assertions green.

bundle_size_budget_75kb.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'bundle size budget (≤ 75 KB gzipped, memo decision 13)': success. Local rebuild from this commit measured the gzipped JS at 15.06 KB (80% headroom under the 76800-byte threshold). Bundle filename index-Bx1CgJqA.js matches the live deployment exactly.

verification.live_bundle_byte_reproducibility: Local `vite build` from commit e090509 produces dist/assets/index-Bx1CgJqA.js — identical filename to the live asset; Vite content-hashes asset names, so identical filenames means identical bytes.

code_review_status: Phase 3.B did not produce new application code; the artifact under verification is the Phase 3.A.2 code already reviewed and approved (artifacts/code-review-phase-3-A-2.md, verdict APPROVE WITH NITS, no blockers). The CI run on commit e090509 also exercised the linter, typechecker, unit tests (563 passing), build, bundle-size gate, and Playwright suite (chromium + firefox + webkit) — every gate passed. No code-reviewer reinvocation required.
qa_playwright_status: Phase 3.B's qa-playwright equivalent is the CI Playwright job (the in-sandbox agent cannot download the Microsoft-hosted browser binaries, and per the established Phase 1.B / 2.B precedent the cross-runtime CI job is the authoritative observation). It ran chromium + firefox + webkit on commit e090509 and reported green for the diagnostic suite (8 tests × 3 engines = 24 passing assertions, including the two new Phase 3.A.2 sim scripted-playthrough assertions).
follow_ups_observations: live-domain-https-enforcement: porkchop.github.io/icefall/ 301-redirects to http://www.aaroncaswell.com/icefall/ (plaintext). Pages allows enforcing HTTPS on a custom domain; consider enabling 'Enforce HTTPS' in the repo's Pages settings as a Phase 9 release-hardening item. Not a Phase 3.B blocker — the deployed bytes and the cross-runtime e2e assertions are independent of transport. The CI Playwright job runs against http://127.0.0.1:4173/icefall/ via vite preview (playwright.config.ts:10,18-19), not against the live URL, so transport choice does not affect the cross-runtime assertion path.
follow_ups_carried_forward_from_phase_3_: harness_mapgen_exception_carry_to_phase_5: When Phase 5's input-driven loop replaces runScripted, relocate the harness's generateFloor + spawnFloorEntities orchestration to src/run/loop.ts (or equivalent) and remove the eslint single-file exception for src/sim/harness.ts.
applyFloorEntry redundant param (run.ts:180-194 takes both newFloor and newFloorState even though newFloorState.floor === newFloor). Cosmetic.
dirOrdinalForStep is exported but used only by tests (ai.ts:169-175). Consider a comment if planned Phase 5+ helper. Optional.
ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_TEXT is imported and re-encoded in combat.ts and combat.test.ts. A shared ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES: Uint8Array constant would remove the re-encoding. Optional.
tick silently treats unknown action types as no-ops (turn.ts:163-165). Consistent with 'additive vocabulary'; a defensive type-check would catch Phase 6+ omissions. Optional.
tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts:6-7 redeclares SIM_DIGEST rather than importing it. Mirrors the pre-existing RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST pattern; intentional for browser-side assertions.
ai.ts:165-166 — defensive 'no valid step found' branch in decideMonsterAction. Unreachable in correct BFS usage; defensive fallback. If exercised in Phase 7+ boss FSM expansion, add a focused test.
turn.ts:225,249-257 — race conditions: stay-with-state-change and move-target-blocked-by-since-moved-monster. Phase 6+ multi-monster combat scenarios may exercise these naturally.
run.ts:55 — rejection-sample re-draw (~1.4e-9 prob/draw). Reachable only with thousands of seeds; consider a stress test in Phase 6 inventory-roll work.

run.ts:141 — items secondary tie-break by kind. Unreachable in Phase 3 (all loot slots emit item.cred-chip). Phase 6 inventory mechanics will exercise this when multiple item kinds spawn at the same tile.
next_phase: Phase 4 — Procedural Atlas Generator (Planning Gate). Per docs/PHASES.md this is a planning-gate phase requiring artifacts/decision-memo-phase-4.md + an architecture-red-team review before any tools/gen-atlas.ts or src/atlas/** code is written. Phase 4's deliverables include the deterministic atlas generator (PNG byte-identical across Linux/macOS/Windows CI runners), the recipe registry, the rulesetVersion = sha256(rulesText ‖ atlasBinaryHash) wiring (which retires the Phase 1 DEV- fingerprint sentinel), and a live atlas preview page. Atlas binary size budget is 256 KB. The decision memo should cover: recipe primitive set (palette ops, dither, noise, masks, glitch ops), how recipes are registered with stable IDs, atlas layout strategy (fixed grid vs packing), and the dev-mode preview UI.
